text.title: Distribution Channel Categories and Mix Strategy
text.summary: Four channel categories (organic/paid/sales-led/viral) with timeline and cost profiles, and portfolio mix rules by MRR stage.

list.item: SEO: 6-12 month timeline, low cost, sustainable low CAC after maturation
Social media organic: 3-6 months, low cost, audience-dependent
Community building (Discord/Slack/forum): 6+ months, low cost, high retention signal
Word of mouth: ongoing, free — only works after retention is proven
Product-led growth (in-product virality): timeline varies, low cost
Search ads (Google, Bing): immediate results, high cost — best for capturing existing demand
Social ads (Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok): immediate, medium-high cost — best for creating demand
Influencer/sponsorships (podcast, newsletter): 1-4 weeks, medium-high cost, hard to attribute
Affiliate: 1-2 month ramp, variable cost (revenue share only)

rule.statement: Keep a "20% experiments" budget non-negotiable even when the primary channel is working — channel concentration risk is the #1 GTM failure mode at $100K+ MRR.
rule.rationale: Channels saturate. Apple ATT, Google cookie deprecation, and algorithm changes have each eliminated primary channels for SaaS companies with no backup. The 20% budget maintains optionality.

list.item: Outbound sales (cold email/call): immediate, high cost, best for high-ACV B2B
Partnerships (co-marketing, integrations): 2-6 month ramp, medium cost
Conferences/events: 1-3 month conversion cycle, high cost
Referral program (incentivized sharing): 1-2 month ramp, variable cost
App marketplace integrations: 2-4 month build, medium cost
Embeds (widgets, badges): 1-2 month build, low cost
User-generated content (templates, showcases): 2-4 months, low cost

rule.statement: Focus on exactly 1 channel from 0 to $10K MRR, expand to 2-3 channels from $10K to $50K MRR, then diversify. 80% effort on 1-2 proven channels, 20% on experiments.
rule.rationale: Trying all channels at once means mastering none. Channel mastery requires iterating 10+ experiments per channel — spreading across 5 channels produces 2 iterations each, which is insufficient for learning the economics.
